content:
# Overall Project Summary (May 2, 2026)

The project focused heavily on stabilizing the framework by resolving critical build compatibility issues across multiple plugins and addressing runtime stability in the core repository. Key achievements include migrating the Jupiter plugin to the official API, fixing headless Linux segfaults, and ensuring ESM-only compatibility by removing broken CommonJS bundles across the plugin ecosystem.

## ✅ Completed Work

### Build System & ESM Compatibility
*   **Plugin ESM Alignment**: Resolved critical dependency conflicts by removing broken CommonJS (CJS) bundles in `plugin-ollama` ([#24](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-ollama/pull/24)), `plugin-openai` ([#25](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-openai/pull/25)), `plugin-openrouter` ([#26](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-openrouter/pull/26)), and `plugin-pdf` ([#9](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-pdf/pull/9)) to ensure integration with the ESM-only `@elizaos/core`.

### API & Infrastructure Migrations
*   **Jupiter API Update**: Successfully migrated `plugin-jupiter` to the official `api.jup.ag` endpoint, implementing mandatory API key authentication and updated build configurations ([elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter#4](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter/pull/4)).

### Runtime Stability & Core Fixes
*   **Environment & Authentication**: Resolved headless Linux segfaults related to D-Bus, fixed Telegram bot race conditions, and corrected Codex CLI authentication logic ([elizaos/eliza#7230](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7230), [elizaos/eliza#7232](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7232), [elizaos/eliza#7238](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7238), [elizaos/eliza#7240](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7240)).
*   **Dependency Upgrades**: Updated core dependencies including `viem` (v2.48.8), `tsdown` (^0.21.0), and Node.js (v24) to improve framework performance ([elizaos/eliza#7237](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7237), [elizaos/eliza#7236](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7236), [elizaos/eliza#7239](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7239)).

## 🏗️ Work in Progress

### New Pull Requests
*   **elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord**: [#50](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord/pull/50) - Fix environment variable handling for `DISCORD_AUTO_REPLY`.
*   **elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram**: [#29](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram/pull/29) - Apply Telegram setup token at runtime.

## 🐞 Issue Triage

### New Issues
*   **elizaos/eliza**:
    *   [#7231](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7231): Runtime crashes on headless Linux due to D-Bus unavailability.
    *   [#7233](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7233): Duplicate `MiladyClient` class definitions.
    *   [#7238](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7238): Incompatibility with modern Codex CLI `auth.json`.
    *   [#7240](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7240): Telegram bot token configuration bridging failure.
    *   [#7241](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7241): Telegraf `bot.launch()` errors under Bun.
    *   [#7243](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7243): Authentication failure due to legacy credential shape checks.
    *   [#7244](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7244): Prototype splitting in `MiladyClient`.
    *   [#7245](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7245): Telegram message loss due to concurrent polling.
*   **elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord**:
    *   [#49](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord/issues/49): Configuration inconsistency regarding `DISCORD_AUTO_REPLY` reading.

### Closed Issues
*   **elizaos/eliza**: [#7238](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7238), [#7243](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7243), [#7240](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7240), [#7233](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7233), [#7244](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7244), [#7241](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7241), [#7245](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7245), [#7231](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7231).
